questions.question: What is the performance feedback assessment designed to do?
options: Serve as a formal two-way communication between a rater and ratee to discuss standards, responsibilities, expectations, and goals
Replace the enlisted performance report with a less formal evaluation method
Provide a one-way communication from the rater to the ratee about deficiencies only
Document only negative performance for administrative action purposes

questions.question: After the performance feedback assessment has been completed, what must the rater do with the original worksheet?
options: File it with the military personnel flight for the official record
Give the original, completed, and signed worksheet to the ratee
Forward it to the senior rater for review and signature
Destroy the original and retain only digital copies

questions.question: When may raters conduct performance feedback assessment sessions by telephone?
options: Whenever the ratee requests a telephone session
When the rater is deployed to a combat zone
In unusual circumstances where face-to-face sessions are impractical
Telephone sessions are never authorized under any circumstances

questions.question: Which performance feedback assessment worksheet is designated for use with enlisted personnel in the grades of AB through TSgt?
options: AF Form 724
AF Form 724-A
AF Form 931
AF Form 932

questions.question: Within how many days of when supervision begins must all initial performance feedback assessment sessions be conducted?
options: 30 days
60 days
90 days
120 days

questions.question: For Chief Master Sergeants and Colonels, what is the only feedback requirement?
options: Both initial and mid-term performance feedback assessments are required
Only a mid-term performance feedback assessment is required
The initial performance feedback assessment is the only feedback required
No performance feedback assessments are required

questions.question: What is the performance evaluation system designed to provide?
options: A tool for commanders to manage personnel assignments exclusively
A reliable, long-term, cumulative record of performance and potential
A short-term snapshot of duty performance only during the current rating period
A mechanism for raters to justify disciplinary actions

questions.question: When a ratee signs an evaluation, what does the signature signify?
options: The ratee concurs with all content and ratings on the evaluation
The ratee waives the right to appeal the evaluation
Acknowledgment of receipt and certification that the ratee has reviewed the evaluation for administrative errors
The ratee agrees that all performance statements are factually accurate

questions.question: What must a performance statement in an evaluation include?
options: A comparison of the ratee's performance to that of their peers
Two elements: the behavior or action taken and the impact, results, or outcome of that behavior or action
A recommendation for the ratee's next assignment or duty position
Three elements: the action, the standard, and the rater's personal assessment

questions.question: If an entry on an evaluation would result in the release of classified information, what should be entered in its place?
options: "Classified" followed by the security classification level
"Redacted for Security" and leave the section blank
"Data Masked"
"See Classified Attachment" with a reference number

questions.question: When documenting adverse information on evaluations, which of the following is considered too vague?
options: "Master Sergeant Smith drove while intoxicated, for which he received an Article 15"
"Captain Jones made improper sexually suggestive and harassing comments to a squadron member, for which he received a Letter of Reprimand"
"An error in judgment led to an off-duty incident"
"Staff Sergeant Doe was convicted of assault under Article 128, UCMJ, and received 30 days extra duty"

questions.question: When do first-term Airmen receive selective reenlistment consideration?
options: Within 12 months of their expiration of term of service
Within 13 months of their expiration of term of service
Within 15 months of their expiration of term of service
Within 18 months of their expiration of term of service

questions.question: If an Airman is not selected for reenlistment, how many calendar days does the Airman have to render an appeal intent?
options: 5 calendar days
3 calendar days
7 calendar days
10 calendar days

questions.question: Who is the appeal authority for first-term Airmen who are non-selected under the Selective Reenlistment Program?
options: The respective wing commander
The Secretary of the Air Force
The respective group commander
The major command commander

questions.question: Under the NCO Career Status Program, Airmen with at least how many years of total active federal military service on date of discharge will be reenlisted for an unspecified period?
options: 8 years
10 years
12 years
15 years

questions.question: What is the maximum voluntary extension per enlistment for all Airmen?
options: 24 months
36 months
48 months
60 months

questions.question: What is the maximum Selective Retention Bonus (SRB) per zone?
Which SRB zone applies to Airmen reenlisting between 18 and 20 years of total active federal military service?
options: Zone A
Zone B
Zone C
Zone E

questions.question: What is the nation's highest unit award?
options: Air Force Outstanding Unit Award
Gallant Unit Citation
Presidential Unit Citation
Joint Meritorious Unit Award

questions.question: What is the highest decoration for heroism in military action that can be awarded to a member of the U.S. Armed Forces?
options: Air Force Cross
Silver Star
Distinguished Service Medal
Medal of Honor

questions.question: What is the second highest decoration that can be awarded to a member of the U.S. Armed Forces?
options: Silver Star
Distinguished Service Medal
Air Force Cross
Legion of Merit

questions.question: The Airman's Medal is awarded for heroism involving voluntary risk of life under what conditions?
options: While engaged in aerial flight operations
While participating in combat against an armed enemy
Conditions other than those of conflict with an armed enemy of the United States
While serving in a designated combat zone during wartime operations

questions.question: What percentage of similar units assigned to a command may be recommended annually for the Air Force Outstanding Unit Award?
options: 5 percent
10 percent
15 percent
20 percent

questions.question: The Purple Heart is a decoration that a member earns entitlement to in what manner?
options: Through nomination by the member's commanding officer
Through recommendation by the member's peers and supervisors
A member earns entitlement rather than being awarded through recommendation
Through a formal board review and approval by the Secretary of the Air Force

questions.question: After the Airman renders appeal intent for Selective Reenlistment Program non-selection, how many calendar days does the Airman have to submit the appeal to the military personnel flight?
options: 5 calendar days
7 calendar days
10 calendar days
15 calendar days
